title: Understanding Vision Transformers: From Attention to ViT
excerpt: A deep dive into how the Transformer architecture was adapted for image recognition tasks, and why it works so well for computer vision.

title: Few-Shot Learning: Teaching AI to Learn from Minimal Examples
excerpt: An accessible guide to few-shot learning paradigms including prototypical networks, MAML, and the role of pre-training in low-data regimes.

title: CNN vs Vision Transformers for Computer Vision
excerpt: A practical comparison of convolutional neural networks and transformer-based architectures for image classification tasks.

title: Incremental Learning: How AI Learns New Classes
excerpt: An overview of continual and incremental learning, including the challenge of catastrophic forgetting in deep learning systems.

title: Domain Adaptation in Computer Vision
excerpt: A simple explanation of how domain adaptation helps models perform better when training and testing environments are different.
